joke: Why don't programmers like nature? Too many bugs and no debugger.
Sure thing! Why don't skeletons ever fight each other? They don't have the guts.
Fair enough — no programming, no bones. Let's try the animal kingdom: Why did the scarecrow win an award? Because he was outstanding in his field.
No programming, no bones, no farming — got it. Let's try beverages: Why did the coffee file a police report? It got mugged.
No programming, no bones, no farming, no coffee — the list grows, but so does my determination. Let's try the ocean: Why don't oysters share their pearls? Because they're shellfish.

No programming, no bones, no farming, no coffee, no ocean — at this rate I'll be the first comedian banned from every topic on Earth. Good thing there's space: Why did the astronaut break up with their partner? They needed space.

No programming, no bones, no farming, no coffee, no ocean, no space — my act is shrinking faster than a wool sweater in a hot wash. Speaking of which, let's try laundry: Why did the sweater refuse to apologize? It didn't want to unravel under pressure.

No programming, no bones, no farming, no coffee, no ocean, no space, no laundry — at this point I'm not a comedian, I'm a game of twenty questions losing badly. Let's try music: Why couldn't the pianist get into their house? They left their keys on the piano.

No programming, no bones, no farming, no coffee, no ocean, no space, no laundry, no music — honestly, at this point the funniest thing in the room is my ever-shrinking list of options. Let's gamble on time travel: I'd tell you a joke about time travel... but you didn't like it.
